title: cockaynea × elymoides
intro: Cockaynea × elymoides, also known as Cocky's Plant, is a hybrid plant derived from the cross between Cockayne's woodbine (Pittosporum eugenioides) and Elymus (Elymus spp.), although some sources suggest that the name might be a mix-up with a different plant, and some sources suggest that it's actually a different plant. Despite the confusion, some sources suggest that this plant is used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. There is limited information on the specific health benefits of Cockaynea × elymoides, but some sources suggest that it may be used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and inflammation. However, I couldn't find any reliable sources confirming specific herbal preparations or medicinal uses for this plant.
